---
title: "Introduction to Robotics"
subtitle: "Temporary Demo Course Website"
---

## Overview

Robotics combines mechanics, electronics, and computation to build autonomous physical systems.
This course introduces the core ideas behind modeling, simulation, planning, control, and perception.

By the end of this course, you will be able to:

- Understand **the fundamentals of robot simulation** (_Left_: MuJoCo, _Right_: IsaacLab)

  ![](videos/home/mujoco_example.mp4){fig-alt="MuJoCo simulation example" width="45%"}
  ![](videos/home/isaac_lab_example.mp4){fig-alt="IsaacLab simulation example" width="45%"}

- Understand **a complete perception-to-action pipeline**, providing the foundation to build real-world robotic systems later

  ![](videos/home/pointing_sim.mp4){fig-alt="Pointing (Sim)" width="45%"}
  ![](videos/home/pointing_real_world.mp4){fig-alt="Pointing (Real World)" width="45%"}<br>
  ![](videos/home/potential_field.mp4){fig-alt="Potentil Field" width="32.4%"}
  ![](videos/home/grasping.mp4){fig-alt="Grasping" width="57.6%"}

## Course Information

- **Instructor:** Yonghyeon Lee
- **Time:** TBA
- **Location:** TBA
- **Credits:** TBA
- **Office Hours:** TBA

## Prerequisites

- Dynamics

## Grading

- Homework: TBA
- Midterm exam: TBA
- Final project/exam: TBA
- Participation: TBA

## Schedule

This schedule is preliminary and may change during the semester.

## Textbook

- TBA

## Resources
- [Lecture Notes](notes/index.qmd)
